original_reasoning: البرشاع=الأهوج الضخم الجافي (reckless, bulky, coarse). λάβρος=furious, boisterous, impetuous. Both describe uncontrolled violent force and wild temperament. Strong conceptual overlap in the domain of fierce impetuosity.
Arabic place-side or flank matches the topographic idea of a promontory with a clear locational chain.
Arabic 'successor/heel/following' matches the conceptual core of sequence; a 'year' is the coming-next cycle.
Arabic glosses of smearing residue and dispersed tears fit the Greek field of pourable liquid or fluid.
Strong onomastic and royal-domain continuity: Arabic lineage context and Greek satrap name closely align.
Arabic ghadar = betrayal/abandonment, ghadir = pool; Greek Gadara = Semitic place name in Palestine (Aramaic/Hebrew root gdr 'wall'). Strong shared Semitic substrate for place name.
Arabic gloss explicitly discusses transmitted reading and wording, while Greek means said; speech-text semantics clearly meet.
Arabic means leveling and broad flatness; Greek rows of olives preserves the ordered laid-out surface concept.
